problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary audit implementation covers git ls-files tracked/cached/other files, includes walkFiles fallback for non-git projects, and excludes only the dispatch artifact root from mutation detection.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates file digests for all project files except allowed artifact roots, using git files or walkFiles fallback
evidence.why: Core boundary snapshot function that captures project state before/after agent execution

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard' to get tracked, cached, and other files excluding standard exclusions
evidence.why: Git-backed file discovery that covers tracked, cached, and other file categories

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively walks all directories except excluded ones (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) for non-git projects
evidence.why: Fallback file discovery for non-git projects that excludes common build/cache dirs

evidence.summary: excludedDir() checks if directory is in excluded list: [.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache]
evidence.why: Defines which directories are excluded from boundary snapshots

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after digests to detect added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: Detects project mutations by comparing file digests

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before agent execution and after, passing outputDir as allowed root to exclude artifacts
evidence.why: Shows boundary snapshots are taken at the right time with proper exclusions

evidence.summary: Boundary changes detected and added to result with project_mutation status and allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Shows how boundary violations are reported

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' - agent creates file, boundary detects violation, blocks with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary blocks project mutations

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' - git init, agent creates untracked file, boundary detects added file
evidence.why: Evidence that git-backed boundary detects untracked mutations

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs' - agent creates .env file, boundary detects added gitignored file
evidence.why: Evidence that gitignored mutations are detected outside artifact roots

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' - agent modifies existing file and creates artifact, boundary detects modification but ignores artifact
evidence.why: Evidence that non-git fallback works and excludes artifacts correctly

evidence.summary: Execution policy declares project_mutation forbidden and specifies allowed_write_roots as [outputDir]
evidence.why: Policy definition that boundary enforcement is documented in dispatch plan

risks: If boundary snapshot coverage has gaps (e.g., certain file types or edge cases), mutations could go undetected
If git ls-files excludes some tracked files by default, those files might not be in the snapshot
missing: Evidence from real Claude -p agent stress testing with actual boundary mutations to confirm detection in production
Evidence that git ls-files -co covers all intended file categories for all git versions and configurations
Evidence that excludedDir list is comprehensive for common project structures

result.notes: Boundary audit implementation is factual and covers both git and non-git projects with proper artifact exclusion. Test coverage confirms detection of tracked, untracked, and gitignored mutations. Main gap is real-world stress testing with actual Claude agents.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence properly distinguishes facts from assumptions, provides verifiable project-scoped evidence with file paths and line numbers, and correctly identifies missing stress-testing evidence as a gap rather than presenting it as factual implementation validation.

evidence.summary: excludedDir() implementation is factual and verifiable in codebase
evidence.why: Explorer correctly identified code location and scope

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard' - exact command is factual
evidence.why: Explorer provided precise git command without assumptions

evidence.summary: walkFiles() fallback is verifiable implementation, not claimed coverage
buildBoundarySnapshot() is verifiable in codebase with exact behavior
Test evidence proves boundary blocks project mutations in test environment
Test evidence proves non-git fallback works and excludes artifacts
Execution policy is documented in code, not assumed
evidence.why: Policy definition is factual code artifact

risks: Real-world Claude agent stress testing gap could miss edge cases not covered by unit tests
Git ls-files behavior across different git versions and configurations is not empirically validated

result.notes: Explorer evidence is high-quality: all claims are backed by verifiable code or test evidence with exact paths and line numbers. Assumptions array is empty because explorer correctly identified gaps as missing items rather than promoting them to facts. Evidence properly distinguishes between implementation existence (fact) and real-world stress validation (missing). The 3 missing items from explorer are appropriate reflections of what cannot be verified from code/tests alone.
